generated_by: INSTALL_AUDIT.md §2 — single source of truth for install.sh, lib/setup.sh, bin/subctl doctor, dashboard /api/settings/install-checks
_notes: Edit ONLY this file when adding/removing/changing a dep.
Bash side reads via jq; TS side via JSON.parse.
name            — display label used in tables
type            — CLI | runtime | platform | service | env | toolchain | GUI | package-manager
tier            — hard | soft   (hard = subctl is broken without it on default config)
why             — one-line reason this is needed
detect          — argv array; exits 0 when present (e.g. ['git','--version'])
fallback_paths  — extra absolute paths to probe if detect fails on PATH
version_min     — null or semver-ish floor (informational; not enforced as a block)
install_method  — preinstalled | brew | brew-cask | curl | npm | npx | manual | homebrew-bootstrap | builtin | walkthrough
install_cmd     — exact command (or short instruction for manual ones)
auto_install    — true if install.sh may run install_cmd unattended (after confirm)
do_not_auto_install — true => detect-only; surface manual_url + skip auto-install
manual_url      — URL operator visits for manual installs (LM Studio, Obsidian)
post_install_hint — printed after install completes (e.g. 'open -a Docker')
depends_on      — id(s) that must be installed first (topological order)

deps.name: macOS / launchd
deps.why: launchctl, PlistBuddy, ~/Library/LaunchAgents, security keychain — all macOS-only

deps.name: bash
deps.why: install.sh shebang + every lib/*.sh; bash 4+ recommended for some lib helpers
deps.install_cmd: preinstalled (default macOS bash 3.2 — 4+ available via: brew install bash)

deps.name: macOS coreutils
deps.why: launchctl, lsof, security, /usr/libexec/PlistBuddy, sips, awk, sed, curl — used across lib/*.sh
detect: command -v launchctl >/dev/null && command -v security >/dev/null && command -v /usr/libexec/PlistBuddy >/dev/null && command -v curl >/dev/null

deps.name: Homebrew
deps.why: auto-install path for jq, tmux, gh, gum, go, docker (cask), obsidian (cask)

deps.name: git
deps.why: subctl_require git in install.sh; lib/update.sh; lib/plugins.sh

deps.name: jq
deps.why: subctl_require jq; threaded through lib/settings.sh, lib/evy.sh, providers, this manifest reader

deps.name: tmux
deps.why: subctl teams claude, every dev-team spawn, watchdog activity polling, dashboard camera-view captures

deps.name: gh
deps.why: components/evy/tools/gh.ts spawnSync; /api/master/diag gh auth status; PR + issue ops in master
deps.post_install_hint: Run: gh auth login   (browser OAuth — not auto-launched mid-install)

deps.name: node
deps.why: npx claude-mem install; npm i -g @openai/codex

deps.name: npm
deps.why: Bundled with node; required for codex install
deps.install_cmd: ships with node — installing node above gives you npm

deps.name: bun
deps.why: dashboard server, master daemon, MCP server, subctl cost. engines.bun >= 1.2
deps.post_install_hint: subctl's ~/.zshrc alias block already sources $HOME/.bun/bin — no manual PATH edit needed.

deps.name: claude
deps.why: providers/claude/auth.sh; every dev-team spawn (master subctl_orch_spawn); dashboard /api/oauth/usage
deps.post_install_hint: After install, authenticate per-account via: subctl auth claude <alias>

deps.name: codex
deps.why: providers/openai/auth.sh; Codex OAuth for OpenAI provider accounts
deps.post_install_hint: Authenticate per-account via: subctl auth codex <alias>

deps.name: docker
deps.why: FOOTHOLD dockerode bridge; first-class hard requirement since v2.0.4
deps.post_install_hint: Open Docker Desktop once to accept system extension prompts: open -a Docker

deps.name: coderabbit
deps.why: components/evy/tools/coderabbit.ts; /api/master/diag coderabbit --version
deps.post_install_hint: Binary lands at ~/.local/bin/coderabbit — make sure that's on PATH.

deps.name: claude-mem
deps.why: Master tier-2 memory worker on http://localhost:37701; SessionStart/Stop observation capture
deps.post_install_hint: Worker indexes after your first claude session in any account.

deps.name: cognee
deps.why: Tier 4 primary memory substrate (Memory Init #1). Knowledge graph engine — components/evy/cognee-client.ts speaks to it at http://127.0.0.1:8745; without it knowledge_graph_* tools are not registered and Tier 4 falls back to claude-mem on :37701.
deps.post_install_hint: Sidecar runs in fallback mode without the cognee Python SDK. For the full graph engine: pip install cognee && launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.subctl.cognee

deps.name: cloakbrowser
deps.why: Stealth Chromium backend for browser-driven verification flows (agent-qa, future browser-use tooling). Source-patched Chromium with humanize mode and CDP sidecar (cloakserve) — used as the trusted browser substrate where stock Playwright gets detected/blocked.
deps.post_install_hint: First launch downloads a ~200MB custom Chromium binary into the cloakbrowser cache (one-time cold-start lag). Library-only — no global CLI shipped; consumers import { launch } from 'cloakbrowser' programmatically. To run the CDP sidecar in Docker: docker run -p 127.0.0.1:9222:9222 cloakhq/cloakbrowser cloakserve

deps.name: LM Studio
deps.why: Default supervisor host (qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b on lmstudio provider); /diag lmstudio check
deps.post_install_hint: After install: open the app → Power user → Developer → "Use lms in terminal" to enable the lms CLI.

deps.name: lms
deps.why: subctl evy supervisor model lifecycle (force-load via /api/v1/models/load)
deps.install_cmd: Open LM Studio → Power user → Developer → "Use lms in terminal"
deps.post_install_hint: Default supervisor model: lms get qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b   (~20GB — install.sh prompts before pulling)

deps.name: obsidian
deps.why: Vault viewer; vault_append writes to ~/Documents/Obsidian Vault/; tier-3 long-form memory
deps.install_cmd: Download from https://obsidian.md (or: brew install --cask obsidian)
deps.post_install_hint: Once installed, configure vault root from dashboard → Settings → Obsidian vault.

deps.name: gum
deps.why: Polished TUI prompts in lib/tui.sh — falls back to numeric prompts without it

deps.name: go
deps.why: Builds bin/subctl-deck (Go TUI session manager). Deck unavailable without it.

deps.name: Telegram bot (evy-notify)
deps.why: subctl evy enable hard-aborts if ~/.config/subctl/evy-notify.json missing (lib/evy.sh:237-258)
deps.post_install_hint: After config exists you can: subctl evy enable

deps.name: Context7 API key
deps.why: Per-account .mcp.json registers Context7; needs CONTEXT7_API_KEY in master process env
deps.install_cmd: Sign up at https://context7.com → copy key → add to ~/.zshrc (export CONTEXT7_API_KEY=...) or master plist EnvironmentVariables

deps.name: CodeWhale (deepseek worker; formerly DeepSeek-TUI)
deps.why: Backs `subctl teams deepseek` workers; only needed if operator runs deepseek-provider accounts. Binary is `codewhale`; Homebrew formula kept the old `deepseek-tui` name.
deps.install_cmd: npm install -g codewhale   (or: cargo install codewhale-cli --locked, or: brew install deepseek-tui)
deps.post_install_hint: Then: subctl accounts add deepseek <alias> <email> && subctl auth deepseek <alias>
